content:
{
    "concerns": [
        {
            "id": "SKP-001",
            "concern": "File-based flock contention on probe cache",
            "severity": "CRITICAL",
            "severity_score": 850,
            "why_matters": "Under high concurrency (e.g., Sprint 4 parallel dispatch), `flock -w 5` on a single per-runtime cache file will cause massive thread starvation, cascading timeouts, and system lockups.",
            "location": "Sprint 1: T1.3",
            "recommendation": "Use a lock-free atomic rename strategy or an in-memory cache layer (e.g., Redis/Memcached) instead of blocking file locks."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-002",
            "concern": "Cross-provider fallback ignores prompt dialect and token limit differences",
            "severity": "CRITICAL",
            "severity_score": 820,
            "why_matters": "Falling back from Anthropic to OpenAI (T3.2) without translating provider-specific prompt formats (e.g., system prompts, tool calling schemas) will result in API rejections or severe quality degradation.",
            "location": "Sprint 3: T3.2 (walk_fallback_chain)",
            "recommendation": "Implement a prompt translation layer or restrict fallback chains to models within the same provider/dialect family."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-003",
            "concern": "Brittle 3-way byte-equality gate for float serialization",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 750,
            "why_matters": "Enforcing byte-for-byte equality across Python, Bash, and TS for float serialization (T2.8) is highly susceptible to minor runtime/interpreter updates, causing constant CI failures and blocking deployments.",
            "location": "Sprint 2: T2.8",
            "recommendation": "Use semantic JSON equivalence testing (e.g., `jq` or a JSON diff tool) rather than strict byte-level string equality."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-004",
            "concern": "Strict linear dependency chain threatens 12-week hard ceiling",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 720,
            "why_matters": "Sprints 1-5 are strictly sequential. A delay in Sprint 1 or 2 (e.g., schema parity issues) will push Sprint 4 and 5 past the 12-week hard ceiling, risking project cancellation or severe scope cuts.",
            "location": "Executive Summary & Appendix A",
            "recommendation": "Decouple Sprint 5 (Smoke Fleet) to run in parallel with Sprints 1-3 using mock adapters, and parallelize schema definitions."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-005",
            "concern": "AWS SigV4 credential expiration race condition with probe cache",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 700,
            "why_matters": "If a probe succeeds and is cached for 60s, but the underlying AWS temporary credentials expire during that window, the subsequent actual invocation will fail, bypassing the probe's protection.",
            "location": "Sprint 2: T2.1 & T2.9 (Bedrock auth)",
            "recommendation": "Include credential expiration checks in the probe cache validation logic, forcing a cache miss if credentials expire within the TTL."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-006",
            "concern": "Sequential fallback on parallel degradation causes upstream timeouts",
            "severity": "MEDIUM",
            "severity_score":
